message:
fix: scope GitHub token to github.com domains to prevent credential leaking on redirects

The downloadFile() and setGitHubAuth() functions unconditionally sent the
Authorization header to any URL, including non-GitHub CDN domains reached
via HTTP redirects. This could leak the GitHub token to third parties.

Changes:
- Add isGitHubHost() to restrict token to github.com and
  githubusercontent.com domains (including subdomains)
- Guard setGitHubAuth() with the domain check
- Add CheckRedirect to downloadFile() that strips the Authorization
  header when following redirects to non-GitHub hosts
- Update tests to validate domain-scoped auth behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
